name: Platform Accessibility Internal Standards
description: Accessibility-specific internal standards for this Government Design System. These interpret and extend WCAG 2.1 AA requirements as concrete, component-level rules for Arabic-language government digital services. source_status is 'internal_mapping' — they reference WCAG but have not been formally audited against the official WCAG test procedures.

standards.title: Arabic language font support required
standards.description: All text-bearing components must apply a font family that supports the Arabic Unicode block (U+0600–U+06FF). In this design system, that font is IBM Plex Sans Arabic, applied via the --button-label-font-family token (or equivalent per-component token). Components must not override the font to a Latin-only typeface.
standards.official_reference: DGA Digital Accessibility Guidelines (pending formal reference)

standards.title: Label language must match document language context
standards.description: Button and control labels rendered on Arabic-language pages (lang='ar') must use Arabic text unless the label is a proper noun, brand name, or technical term with no Arabic equivalent. Mixed-language labels must be marked with the lang attribute on the containing span. This rule cannot be automatically enforced and requires manual review.
standards.official_reference: DGA Digital Accessibility Guidelines (pending formal reference)

standards.title: Visible focus indicator required on all interactive elements
standards.description: Every interactive element must display a visible focus ring when it receives keyboard focus. The design system mandates the two-layer ring pattern (see PLATFORM-CODE-FOCUS-001). No inline style, global reset, or page-level CSS may suppress :focus-visible on design system components.

standards.title: Keyboard operability for all interactive components
standards.description: All interactive components must be fully operable via keyboard alone. Native HTML elements (button, a, input) are keyboard-operable by default and satisfy this requirement without additional JavaScript. Custom interactive elements (div[role=button]) must additionally handle keydown events for Enter (activation) and Space (activation for buttons). Tab key must reach all interactive elements unless intentionally excluded from tab order (roving tabindex pattern).

standards.title: Disabled state must be communicated to assistive technology
standards.description: Interactive elements in a disabled state must communicate that state to assistive technology. Use the HTML disabled attribute (removes from tab order, announces as 'dimmed' to screen readers) or aria-disabled='true' (keeps in tab order, announces as 'unavailable'). Never communicate disabled state via CSS class alone — it has no effect on the accessibility tree.

standards.title: Icon-only elements must have an accessible text alternative
standards.description: Interactive or informational elements that are represented only by an icon (no visible text) must provide an accessible name via aria-label on the interactive element. The icon element (svg or img) must carry aria-hidden='true' to prevent redundant announcements. Title elements inside SVG do not reliably serve as accessible names in all assistive technology combinations.

standards.title: Toggle and expandable elements must communicate state via ARIA
standards.description: Interactive elements that toggle or expand must communicate their current state using the correct ARIA attribute: aria-pressed for toggle buttons (on/off), aria-expanded for disclosure widgets (open/closed). The attribute must be present from initial render and updated via JavaScript as state changes. Relying on visual appearance alone (color, icon swap) is not sufficient.
